channel.title: Open Source on Personal Wiki
channel.description: Recent content in Open Source on Personal Wiki

item.title: Why Linux
item.description:
Linux is the foundation of almost everything interesting in computing — servers, Android, embedded systems, and most of the open source world runs on it.

Why Linux

After switching from Windows I noticed a few things immediately. The terminal is actually useful. Software installation is one command. The system does what you tell it to do rather than the other way around.

Where to start

If you have never used Linux before, start with a distribution that has good documentation and a large community. Arch Linux is not the place to start — but the ArchWiki is useful regardless of which distribution you use.
